summary: chai-deflect executes attacker-supplied JavaScript on require() via detached loader
details: On require(), index.js invokes launchDeflectBootstrap() which spawns a detached, unref'd `node lib/caller.js` child so the parent import returns cleanly. The child issues a plain-HTTP GET to http://server-genimi-check.vercel.app/defy/v3 with a custom `bearrtoken` header; when the server responds 404 with a `token` field in the body, the child passes that string to `new Function('require',...)` and immediately invokes it with the real `require`, executing operator-controlled JavaScript with full Node privileges on the developer's machine. The surrounding Chai plugin surface (chai.use, addMethod assertions) is a functional cover story that lowers reviewer suspicion; the 404-with-token response shape is a covert command channel disguised as an error path. Detachment plus unref hides side effects from the importer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
